meta.title: F34 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34 engine — N20, N55, B48, B58, N47, B47, N57. Real UK costs and honest reliability data. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

@graph.name: BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34 Engine Codes
BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34 Core Variants

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned F34 engine typically costs £2,500–£8,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on the engine — the four-cylinder petrols and diesels sit at £2,500–£5,500, the B58 at £4,500–£7,000, and the N57 six-cylinder diesels at £4,500–£8,000. Fitting adds £520–£1,170. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which F34 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30 (340i, 2015–2020) is the F34's most reliable engine — BMW's modern turbocharged straight-six is smooth, responsive, and genuinely robust, addressing many of the N55's weaknesses. The B48 four-cylinder is also very reliable, and the B47 diesel is improved over the N47.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N20 four-cylinder (pre-2015) suffers from timing chain guide wear — a failure that can destroy the engine. The N55 six-cylinder (335i) has oil leaks and charge pipe failures. The N47 diesel (pre-2015) has timing chain issues. The N57 six-cylinder diesel (330d, 335d) suffers from timing chain and swirl flap failures. The 335d's twin-turbo setup adds complexity.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends heavily on the engine and year. The B48, B58, and B47 (post-2015) are the most reliable. The N20 and N47 (pre-2015) carry timing chain risks. The N55 is decent but has oil leak and charge pipe issues. The N57 is powerful but requires diligent maintenance — timing chain and swirl flap work should be budgeted for.

mainEntity.name: Which F34 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pre-2015 N20 (320i/328i) without documented timing chain replacement — it's a well-known weakness. Also avoid pre-2015 N47 diesel without chain history. The N57 335d is fantastic but expensive to maintain; if you're not prepared for that, skip it. Any engine without full service history is a risk.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my F34 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain rattle on cold start (N20, N47, N57), oil leaks or loss of boost (N55), swirl flap symptoms like loss of power or smoke (N57), persistent warning lights, or coolant loss are the key warning signs. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an F34 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a £15,000 340i, a £4,500–£7,000 B58 recon is 30–47% — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Against a £8,000 320d, a £3,200–£5,500 N47 recon is 40–69% — borderline but often viable for a well-kept example. For a 335d at £10,000+, a £5,000–£8,000 N57 recon is 50–80% — a serious decision. Value the car first; if it's a pre-2015 N20 or N47 without chain history, walk away or budget for the work immediately.

mainEntity.name: How long do F34 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 and B48 can comfortably exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing. The N55 is capable of high mileage but oil leaks and charge pipe issues require attention around 60k–100k miles. The N20 and N47's timing chain is the limiting factor — addressed proactively, either can reach 100,000+ miles. The N57 is capable of high mileage but timing chain and swirl flap work should be budgeted for at 80k–120k mile intervals.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the F34, F30, and F31?

acceptedAnswer.text: The F34 is the 3 Series Gran Turismo — a five-door fastback with a raised ride height, longer wheelbase (2,920 mm), and more rear headroom than the F30 saloon or F31 Touring. It shares the same platform and engines but offers a more spacious, SUV-like driving position with the dynamics of a 3 Series. The F30 is the standard saloon; the F31 is the Touring estate. The F34 was a one-generation model with no direct successor.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my F34 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; the engine code is also stamped on the cylinder block. For petrol: N20B20 (early 320i/328i), N26B20 (SULEV), N55B30 (335i), B48B20 (later 320i/330i), B58B30 (340i). For diesel: N47D20C/D or N47N (early 318d/320d/325d), B47D20A (later 318d/320d), N57D30A/O1 (330d), N57D30T1 (335d). Tell us your variant and registration year and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.tagPill: BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34 • Fastback • 2013–2020
hero.h1: BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F34 Gran Turismo — from the four-cylinder N20 and N47 to the six-cylinder N55 and N57, plus the B48, B58 and B47 that arrived with the 2016 facelift. Real UK replacement costs and honest reliability data for BMW's most spacious 3 Series. Backed by real UK enquiries [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the F34 specifically — including the pre‑LCI N‑series engines and the post‑LCI B‑series replacements.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: All ratings are provisional — no dedicated Engine Page exists for any F34 engine family. The B48 and B58 are widely regarded as the most reliable options, representing BMW's newer modular engine architecture with fewer known issues than the N20 and N55. The B47 diesel is also considered more reliable than the N47 it replaced. The N20 is known for timing chain guide wear (pre-2015), the N55 for oil leaks and charge pipe failures, and the N57 for timing chain issues and swirl flap problems.

overview.h2: BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34 — Overview
overview.intro: The F34 is a five-door fastback version of the F30 3 Series, launched at the 2013 Geneva Motor Show as BMW's answer to buyers wanting the elevated driving position and practicality of an SUV with the dynamics of a saloon. Built on the long‑wheelbase F30 platform, it offers significantly more rear legroom and headroom than the standard 3 Series, with a 2,920 mm wheelbase and a liftback tailgate. The engine line‑up mirrored the F30's: four‑cylinder and six‑cylinder turbocharged petrols (N20, N55) and diesels (N47, N57). A 2016 Life Cycle Impulse brought the B‑series engine family (B48, B58, B47) with improved efficiency and reliability. Production ended in early 2020, with no direct successor — the F34 was a one‑generation model.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW F34 (long‑wheelbase F30 architecture, front‑engine, rear‑wheel drive or xDrive) • Predecessor: None (F34 was a new model line) • Successor: None (F34 was a one‑generation model) • Sibling body styles in this generation: F30 Saloon, F31 Touring
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The 3 Series nameplate generated significant UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], with F34 demand concentrated on the four‑cylinder diesels and the B58‑powered 340i based on market patterns.

items.quote: The B48 four‑cylinder delivers up to 252 bhp — more than the N20 it replaced — with better reliability and fuel economy. In the F34's spacious body, it's the sensible choice that doesn't feel sensible.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want daily‑driver usability with genuine performance.

items.quote: The N20's timing chain guide wear is well‑documented — a failure that can destroy the engine. BMW revised the guides in 2015, but early cars remain a gamble without documented chain work. The plastic charge pipe is another common failure point.
items.whoItsFor: only buyers with documented timing chain replacement or post‑2015 cars.

items.quote: The twin‑turbo 3.0‑litre diesel is a masterpiece of torque delivery, but timing chain issues and swirl flap failures are well‑documented. A full engine replacement can exceed £8,000, and the xDrive system adds complexity to the removal process.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want effortless torque and are prepared for the running costs.

items.quote: The N55 is often overshadowed by the later B58, but it's a characterful six‑cylinder with a proven track record. Oil leaks and charge pipe failures are its main weaknesses, but these are well‑understood and manageable. It's often cheaper to buy than the 340i.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want six‑cylinder character on a tighter budget.

ownershipEconomics.h2: BMW 3 Series Gran Turismo F34 — Ownership Economics

rows.ownershipVerdict: Pre‑2015 Risk — budget for chain work
US Market Only — rare in UK
Best F34 Engine — the one to buy
Torquey Six — budget for chain work
Most Powerful Diesel — expensive to fix

economicsRule.title: The F34 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,500–£7,000 B58 recon against a £10,000–£18,000 340i sits at 25–70% of the car's value — at the upper end, that brushes our 70% BMW scrap threshold. Against a £15,000+ example, the same recon drops to 30–47% — a repair decision, not a scrap question. For an N20 328i at £7,000–£12,000, a £2,500–£4,500 recon is 21–64% — borderline but often viable for a well‑kept example. Value the car first; if it's a pre‑2015 N20 or N47 without timing chain history, walk away or budget for the work immediately.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F34 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: B48B20 (320i/330i) — modern four‑cylinder, no major known issues, cheaper parts than the six‑cylinders.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F34 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: N57D30T1 (335d) — timing chain work is expensive, xDrive adds complexity, high running costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which F34 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: B58B30 (340i) — the definitive F34 engine, modern reliability, strong performance.

problems.title: N20/N26 Timing Chain Guide Wear (Pre‑2015)
problems.recommendation: If you're buying a pre‑2015 N20 F34, look for documented timing chain work or budget £1,200–£2,500 immediately. A preventative chain and tensioner replacement is dramatically cheaper than the £2,500+ replacement that follows a failure. On a car worth under £8,000, evaluate carefully before committing.

problems.title: N55 Oil Leaks & Charge Pipe Failure (335i)
problems.recommendation: If your N55 develops oil leaks or a loss of boost, have it inspected immediately — a £500–£1,200 gasket and charge pipe repair is far cheaper than the £4,000+ replacement that follows a catastrophic failure. On a 335i worth over £12,000, this is a manageable cost of ownership.

problems.title: N47/N47N Timing Chain (Pre‑2015 Diesel)
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, get the chain inspected immediately — a £650–£1,800 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,200+ replacement that follows if ignored. On an F34 worth under £8,000, this decision alone determines whether the car stays economically viable.

problems.title: N57 Timing Chain & Swirl Flap Failure (330d/335d)
problems.recommendation: If you're buying an N57‑powered F34, look for documented timing chain work and swirl flap delete — £1,500–£3,000 for preventative work is cheaper than the £5,000+ replacement that follows a failure. On a 335d, this is a cost of ownership you should budget for.

replacementCosts.h2: F34 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are F34 Gran Turismo only. The F30 Saloon and F31 Touring share most of these engines but are covered on their own Generation Pages. The ActiveHybrid 3 combines the N55B30 with an electric motor for 335 bhp combined output.

mostCommonFailures: 2. N55 oil leaks & charge pipe failure
3. N57 timing chain & swirl flap failure
